title: machaonia erythrocarpa subsp. erythrocarpa
intro: Machaonia erythrocarpa subsp. erythrocarpa, also known as the Guanacaste tree or Guanacaste shrub, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. Its health benefits include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts are also used in pharmaceutical formulations, making it a valuable resource in herbal medicine.
